title: A successful invocation exits 0
rationale: Exit status is how scripts and agents detect success. A successful run (such as --help or --version) must exit 0.
remediation: Return exit code 0 from successful runs; reserve non-zero codes for failures.

title: Errors exit non-zero
rationale: An unknown flag or misuse is an error; exiting 0 anyway makes failures invisible to scripts and CI.
remediation: Use an argument parser that rejects unknown flags and exits with a non-zero status.

title: Help and primary output go to stdout
rationale: Primary output belongs on stdout so it can be piped; messaging and errors belong on stderr. Help text is primary output.
remediation: Print help and normal results to stdout; send logs and errors to stderr.
